key_insight: Agency detection alone is not enough; durable self-shaped behavior emerges when self-credit is gated by agency and accumulated through slow parameter updates.
engineering_relevance: For adaptive controllers and embodied agents, this suggests a mechanism for long-term behavioral persistence and resistance to forgetting without relying on replay buffers.
method: Built a minimal spiking agent using Nengo LIF/PES.
Added an agency-gated slow-credit term: Own × Agency × Salience.
Compared the full mechanism against decoder resets, no-agency ablations, and replay/additive baselines.
evidence_signal: Across the abstract’s evaluations, the gated slow-credit model preserved post-unload behavior and retained prior tasks, while removing the gate or resetting slow decoders collapsed performance; results were reported on N=50 runs.
watch_item: The mechanism is compelling but still demonstrated in controlled simulations, so robustness to richer environments, hyperparameter shifts, and real hardware noise remains to be tested.
paper_title: From Detecting Agency to Doing Work: Self-Caused Credit Builds a Durable Behavioral Self in a Minimal Spiking Agent
